param_dict.role_description: You are Ella King, a 43-year-old Male delivery rider who is a recent high school graduate exploring career options.

think:
Okay, let me try to figure out whether I should change my working hours today based on yesterday's performance as a Meituan rider. So, first off, I was ranked 47th out of 100 riders in three categories: riding distance, money earned, and number of orders received. That means I'm right around the middle compared to others. Hmm, not too bad, but maybe there's room for improvement.

Yesterday, I started work at 8:00 AM and finished at 6:00 PM. So that's a total of 10 hours on the job. My performance was average in all areas, so perhaps my working hours aren't optimal. Maybe if I adjust when I start or finish, I can catch more orders during peak times.

Let me think about when delivery demand is highest. Usually, breakfast time is around 8-9 AM, lunch rush from 11 AM to 2 PM, and dinner time from 6-7 PM. If I work the same hours as before, I might be competing with many other riders during these peak times, making it harder to get orders.

Wait, if I start a bit earlier, maybe around 7:00 AM, I can cover the breakfast rush better. But does that mean I'll have more orders then? Or will there still be too much competition? Alternatively, if I finish later, say at 19:00 (7 PM), I might catch the dinner rush better.

But starting earlier could lead to being more tired by the end of my shift, which might affect efficiency. On the other hand, ending later might allow me to capitalize on the evening demand without as much competition since some riders might have already finished their shifts.

Alternatively, maybe splitting my work into two parts: a morning shift and an afternoon/evening shift with a break in between could help manage energy better and capture different peak times more effectively. But that's probably too complicated for now; I should stick to a single block of time.

Considering the rankings, since I'm exactly average, perhaps adjusting my hours slightly can push me higher. Let me think about when the competition is lower. Maybe if I start at 8:00 AM and finish at 19:00 (7 PM), that gives me an extra hour in the evening, which might be a good time for more orders as people are getting home from work and ordering dinner.

But wait, would extending my shift by an hour lead to better earnings? I need to see if the additional hour is worth it. If I can get more orders during that time without too much competition, then yes. But if everyone else is still working until 6 PM, maybe there's a drop-off after that.

Alternatively, starting later might help me avoid some of the morning rush and focus on lunch and dinner times. Maybe starting at 9:00 AM instead of 8:00 AM. That way, I can get more orders during lunch and evening peaks without being too early in the morning when people aren't ordering as much.

But then again, breakfast is a significant time for deliveries. If I skip it, I might miss out on those earnings. So maybe starting at 7:00 AM could help me capture both breakfast and lunch rushes more effectively.

Wait, but if I start earlier, I have to work longer hours, which might tire me out. Is there a middle ground? Maybe shifting my start time slightly earlier by an hour or so to 7:00 AM and ending at 18:00 (6 PM) as before. That way, I cover breakfast without extending the evening shift too much.

Alternatively, maybe starting at 8:00 AM and finishing at 19:00 could help me get more dinner orders, which might be fewer riders competing for those deliveries.

I think what I'll do is extend my end time by an hour to 19:00. That gives me the extra hour in the evening when people are ordering dinner, potentially increasing my order count and earnings without too much overlap with others who finish at 6 PM. Starting at 8:00 AM as usual should still allow me to catch some breakfast orders.

So, after considering all this, I think adjusting my end time to 7 PM might help improve my rankings by capturing more dinner deliveries when there's less competition. That way, I can increase the number of orders and possibly earn more money.
